loyaltyMetaDescription: Member loyalty overview, discovery, and business detail surfaces.
memberAuthRequiredRouteSummaryMessage: This route belongs to the authenticated member portal. Sign in or create an account first, then continue to the protected destination instead of falling back to a minimal access block.
memberAuthRequiredFollowUpMessage: Protected entry points should still stay connected to the wider front-office system, so you can continue through storefront and account surfaces before returning to the authenticated route.
accountHubTitle: Public self-service flows are now split from the future member session.
accountHubDescription: Registration, activation, and password recovery can move forward now. Full profile, addresses, and authenticated member navigation remain intentionally blocked on the final browser auth/session decision.
accountHubReadinessMessage: Cart status: {cartStatus}. Cart lines: {cartLineCount}. CMS status: {cmsStatus}. Category status: {categoriesStatus}. Public account entry should keep the current storefront state visible before the shopper crosses into sign-in or registration.
accountHubReadinessCartEmpty: No active storefront cart is currently available.
accountHubActionCenterMessage: Public account entry should still help the shopper move through live commerce and content surfaces instead of stopping at auth-only cards.
accountHubActionCartDescription: {itemCount} active lines totaling {total} are ready for another checkout attempt.
accountHubActionCartFallbackDescription: No active cart is visible right now, so the next best move is to sign in and reopen the intended storefront route.
accountHubActionCmsFallbackDescription: Published storefront content is available through the current public CMS contract.
accountHubActionCatalogFallbackDescripti: Continue storefront discovery from the public catalog even before the auth step completes.
accountHubRouteMapTitle: Move across public account entry routes
accountHubRouteMapDescription: Account self-service should stay connected to the wider storefront system, so public account entry does not become an isolated auth island.
accountHubHomeDescription: Go back to the composed storefront entry surface to review the broader CMS, catalog, and member system state.
accountHubCatalogDescription: Carry public account entry back into browse and product discovery without leaving the shared storefront shell.
accountHubCmsDescription: Move from self-service entry into public CMS pages so content, commerce, and account entry stay visible as one front-office system.
publicAuthStorefrontWindowMessage: Cart status: {cartStatus}. Cart lines: {cartLineCount}. CMS status: {cmsStatus}. Category status: {categoriesStatus}. Visible CMS pages: {pageCount}. Visible categories: {categoryCount}. Public auth and self-service routes should stay connected to live cart continuity plus published content and catalog browse follow-up instead of behaving like isolated auth leaves.
publicAuthCartDescription: {itemCount} cart lines are still active, totaling {total}. Current cart status: {status}.
publicAuthCartFallbackDescription: No active cart lines are currently visible, but storefront checkout continuity should still stay reachable from public account entry.
publicAuthCartEmptyMessage: No live storefront cart is currently visible from public account entry. Current status: {status}.
publicAuthReturnCartStateEmpty: No active storefront cart is currently available.
publicAuthReturnAccountDescription: After sign-in or registration, this flow returns to the member account landing route so the protected portal can open from a stable entry surface.
publicAuthReturnCheckoutDescription: This auth flow was entered from storefront checkout. After the auth step completes, the shopper should fall back into checkout instead of losing the active conversion path.
publicAuthReturnCartDescription: This auth flow was entered from the storefront cart. After auth succeeds, the shopper should return to cart and continue toward checkout.
publicAuthReturnOrdersDescription: This auth flow was entered from a protected order route. After auth succeeds, the member should return to the relevant order surface instead of restarting from the dashboard.
publicAuthReturnInvoicesDescription: This auth flow was entered from a protected invoice route. After auth succeeds, the member should return to the relevant invoice surface instead of restarting from the dashboard.
publicAuthReturnLoyaltyDescription: This auth flow was entered from a loyalty route. After auth succeeds, the member should return to the loyalty surface that triggered the sign-in wall.
publicAuthReturnGenericDescription: This auth flow preserves a sanitized app-local destination. After auth succeeds, the browser should continue to {returnPath}.
publicAuthCmsFallbackDescription: Published storefront content is available through the current public CMS contract.
publicAuthCmsEmptyMessage: No CMS spotlight pages are currently visible from public account entry. Current status: {status}.
publicAuthCatalogFallbackDescription: Continue storefront discovery from this currently visible public category.
publicAuthCatalogEmptyMessage: No public category lanes are currently visible from public account entry. Current status: {status}.
sessionStrategyNoteDescription: Login, profile editing, address book, orders, invoices, and loyalty remain behind the browser-session decision. This keeps Darwin.Web from hard-coding a token transport that the wider platform has not yet finalized.
cardSignInTitle: Sign in to the protected portal
cardSignInDescription: Use the provisional browser session layer to open profile, orders, invoices, and loyalty pages in the web portal.
cardRegisterDescription: Consumer self-service registration is now available directly against the member auth contract.
cardActivationDescription: The current backend activation lifecycle is now exposed in the storefront instead of staying implicit.
cardPasswordTitle: Request or complete a password reset
cardPasswordDescription: Password reset now stays on the public web route structure while still using the existing backend email/token flow.
registerDescription: This screen registers a member directly through the public member auth endpoint and then relies on the existing backend confirmation-email lifecycle.
registrationSubmittedMessage: The account was created. Check the email confirmation flow before expecting sign-in to succeed.
currentBoundaryRegistration: Registration is live, but browser sign-in persistence is still a separate decision.
currentBoundaryCommunication: Confirmation email behavior stays on the backend Communication Core path.
activationDescription: This route exposes the existing backend confirmation lifecycle to the storefront without inventing a web-only activation system.
activationRequestedMessage: If the account exists, the activation email has been requested through the current backend flow.
activationConfirmedMessage: The email confirmation request completed successfully. Browser sign-in is still a separate session decision.
activationRecoveryDescription: Public auth routes should not leave activation recovery hidden behind a separate route. Request another confirmation email here or continue into the dedicated activation flow with the same return path.
activationEmailRequiredMessage: Email is required to request confirmation.
activationRequestFailedMessage: Activation email could not be requested.
activationEmailTokenRequiredMessage: Email and token are both required to confirm the account.
activationConfirmFailedMessage: Email confirmation could not be completed.
passwordRecoveryTitle: Request or complete a password reset
passwordRecoveryDescription: This route exposes the existing backend reset-token lifecycle and keeps the web client out of its own private reset implementation.
passwordRequestedMessage: If the account exists, the password-reset email has been requested through the current backend flow.
passwordResetMessage: The password reset completed successfully. Browser sign-in remains a separate session decision.
passwordRequestEmailRequiredMessage: Email is required to request a password reset.
passwordRequestFailedMessage: Password reset request could not be completed.
passwordResetFieldsRequiredMessage: Email, token, and new password are required.
signInDescription: This first browser-auth slice uses web-owned cookies to hold the member session while keeping all protected data behind the canonical member endpoints.
memberAuthHttpErrorMessage: The member auth request could not be completed.
memberAuthNetworkErrorMessage: Member auth API could not be reached.
memberSessionUnauthorizedMessage: The member session is no longer authorized.
memberResourceNotFoundMessage: The requested member resource was not found.
memberApiHttpErrorMessage: The member API request could not be completed.
memberApiInvalidPayloadMessage: The member API returned an invalid response payload.
memberApiNetworkErrorMessage: The member API could not be reached.
sessionNoteArchitecture: This is a provisional browser-session layer owned by Darwin.Web, not a permanent auth architecture decision.
sessionNoteApi: Orders, invoices, loyalty, and profile data still come only from Darwin.WebApi.
memberDataWarningsTitle: Some member data loaded with warnings.
memberDashboardDescription: This dashboard reads the authenticated member profile surfaces through the new web-owned browser session layer.

profileUnavailable: Profile data is not currently available for this member session.
preferencesUnavailable: Preference data is not currently available for this member session.
portalRoutesDescription: Use the same member-portal navigation across dashboard, profile, preferences, addresses, orders, invoices, and loyalty so these surfaces behave like one system.
ordersPortalNote: Order history now sits inside the same member-portal navigation as profile, preferences, addresses, invoices, and loyalty so post-purchase follow-up is not a detached route.
invoicesPortalNote: Invoice history now shares the same member-portal navigation and should behave like part of the same billing and account surface instead of a separate archive page.
orderDetailPortalNote: Order detail stays inside the shared member-portal navigation so payment retry, shipments, invoices, and account follow-up remain connected.
invoiceDetailPortalNote: Invoice detail stays inside the shared member-portal navigation so billing follow-up, linked orders, and account context remain connected.

orderDetailReadinessPaymentHealthy: No payment retry is currently needed

dashboardCommunicationWindowMessage: Profile status: {profileStatus}. Preferences status: {preferencesStatus}. Channel readiness should stay visible on the dashboard instead of hiding behind the preferences route.

dashboardActionSecurityInvalidSessionDes: The current browser session does not expose a valid expiry timestamp. Review password and session readiness from the security route.

memberCrossSurfaceRailTitle: Move across connected member and storefront routes
memberCrossSurfaceMessage: Member routes should stay connected to the wider front-office system so account, commerce, and loyalty do not feel like isolated islands.
memberCrossSurfaceHomeDescription: Go back to the composed storefront entry surface to review the broader CMS, catalog, and member system state.
memberCrossSurfaceCatalogDescription: Carry member follow-up back into browse and product discovery without leaving the shared front-office shell.
memberCrossSurfaceAccountDescription: Return to the account dashboard to continue profile, preferences, and address follow-up from the canonical member portal.
memberCrossSurfaceOrdersDescription: Move into authenticated post-purchase follow-up without leaving the shared member portal model.
memberCrossSurfaceInvoicesDescription: Continue into billing follow-up and document access through the same authenticated front-office shell.
memberCrossSurfaceLoyaltyDescription: Continue into discovery, rewards, and business follow-up without treating loyalty like a detached side journey.
profileUpdatedMessage: The member profile was saved through the canonical profile endpoint.
profileNeedsAttentionMessage: The member profile endpoint returned status "{status}".
noProfileEditMessage: No profile snapshot is currently available for editing.
profileRouteSummaryMessage: Profile status: {status}. Editing should stay connected to preference and address follow-up instead of behaving like an isolated form route.
profileRouteSummaryUnavailableMessage: Profile editing is currently unavailable with status "{status}", but this route should still guide the member to adjacent account follow-up surfaces.
boundaryProfileMessage: Email remains display-only here. This page only edits the fields exposed by the canonical member profile contract.
phoneVerificationTitle: Keep phone status aligned with the canonical profile contract
memberPaymentHandoffMissingUrlMessage: Hosted checkout URL was not returned.

memberPaymentHandoffUnreachableMessage: The payment handoff endpoint could not be reached.
profileRequiredFieldsMessage: Profile update is missing required fields.
preferencesRowVersionRequiredMessage: Preference update requires a row version.
phoneAlreadyVerifiedMessage: This member profile is already marked as phone-number confirmed.
phoneCodeRequestedMessage: The backend accepted the phone verification request. The member should receive a code through the selected channel.
phoneVerifiedMessage: The verification code was accepted and the member profile should now reflect a confirmed phone number.
phoneMissingForVerificationMessage: Save a phone number on the profile first before requesting a verification code.
phoneVerificationUnavailable: Phone verification becomes available once the member profile can be loaded.
profileReadinessMessage: Profile status: {status}. Identity, phone, and locale completeness should stay visible from the profile route instead of being inferred only from the edit form itself.

preferencesNeedsAttentionMessage: The member preferences endpoint returned status "{status}".
noPreferencesEditMessage: No preference snapshot is currently available for editing.
currentContractPreferencesMessage: These switches map directly to the current member preference contract. More granular communication controls can be layered later when Communication Core grows.
preferencesRouteSummaryMessage: Preference editing is live and should stay aligned with profile and address follow-up so member communication controls do not drift into a dead-end settings page.
preferencesRouteSummaryUnavailableMessag: Preference editing is not currently available, but this route should still connect the member back to profile and address follow-up.
preferencesRouteSummaryProfileMessage: Preference status: {preferencesStatus}. Profile status: {profileStatus}. Communication preferences should stay aligned with profile/channel readiness instead of drifting into a detached toggle screen.
preferencesChannelReadinessMessage: Profile status: {profileStatus}. Preference status: {preferencesStatus}. Channel toggles only become fully usable when the matching profile channel is actually available.
preferencesEmailChannelReadyMessage: Email delivery can use {email} when email marketing is enabled.
preferencesEmailChannelUnavailableMessag: No usable email address is currently visible from the canonical member profile.

preferencesSmsChannelVerificationMessage: A phone number exists, but the canonical profile still needs phone verification before SMS preferences are fully ready.

preferencesWhatsAppChannelVerificationMe: A phone number exists, but the canonical profile still needs phone verification before WhatsApp preferences are fully ready.
preferencesWhatsAppChannelUnavailableMes: No verified phone channel is currently available for WhatsApp preference follow-up.
securityEditDescription: This route keeps password change inside the authenticated member portal and uses the canonical authenticated password-change endpoint instead of falling back to a public reset flow.
securityUpdatedMessage: The current member password was changed through the canonical authenticated endpoint.
securityRouteSummaryMessage: Password change should stay inside the authenticated portal, close to profile and preference follow-up, instead of sending the member back through public recovery routes for an active-session task.
securityBoundaryMessage: This route changes the password for the current authenticated member session. It does not replace the public reset flow for locked-out or email-recovery scenarios.
securityRequiredFieldsMessage: Current password, new password, and confirmation are required.
securityConfirmMismatchMessage: The new password and confirmation must match.
addressesRouteSummaryMessage: Address status: {status}. {count} saved addresses are currently visible on this route.

addressUpdateIdentifiersMessage: Address update requires id and row version.

loyaltyOverviewTitleSignedOut: Loyalty discovery is available before a member joins
loyaltyOverviewDescriptionSignedIn: Joined-business dashboards still come from the authenticated member contracts, while discovery and pre-join business context now come from the public business contracts.
loyaltyOverviewDescriptionSignedOut: Anonymous visitors can browse loyalty-ready businesses first, then sign in only when they want to join a program or access member-only balance and history screens.

loyaltyMembershipCreatedMessage: The canonical member join contract completed. This route should now fall through to the full member dashboard on reload.
programSnapshotDescription: Reward tiers now come from the public loyalty-program contract, so pre-join storefront UX can explain value before the member enrolls.

joinReadinessAnonymous: Sign in first to create a loyalty account for this business.

discoveryDescriptionDefault: Public discovery now uses the canonical business-discovery contracts and keeps loyalty-ready businesses visible even before the member joins.
discoveryDescriptionSignedIn: The joined-business list remains member-specific, but discovery now stays visible so the portal can also act as a growth surface for the next place the customer wants to join.
discoveryDescriptionSignedOut: This discovery list is public and contract-driven, so storefront visitors can inspect businesses and reward tiers before authenticating.

invoicesStorefrontWindowMessage: CMS status: {cmsStatus}. Category status: {categoriesStatus}. Visible CMS pages: {pageCount}. Visible categories: {categoryCount}. Invoice history should stay connected to public content and catalog follow-up instead of behaving like a portal-only finance archive.
invoicesStorefrontCmsFallbackDescription: Published storefront content is available through the current public CMS contract.
invoicesStorefrontCmsEmptyMessage: No CMS spotlight pages are currently visible from invoice history. Current status: {status}.
invoicesStorefrontCatalogFallbackDescrip: Continue storefront discovery from this currently visible public category.
invoicesStorefrontCatalogEmptyMessage: No public category lanes are currently visible from invoice history. Current status: {status}.
noInvoicesMessage: No member invoices were returned for this history page.
invoiceDetailUnavailableMessage: The member invoice endpoint returned status "{status}".
paymentSummaryDescription: Billing presentation now consumes the canonical summary fields and document link exposed by the member invoice contract.
